items.title: Donald Trump and Xi Jinping are meeting in China but only one has the upper hand

items.title: Canberra to Sydney rail link gets $100 million upgrade to improve train speeds
items.summary: The federal, ACT and NSW governments will spend $100 million upgrading the Canberra to Sydney rail link in a bid to reduce the train trip to under four hours.

items.title: Aboriginal man expresses remorse after avoiding jail for vandalising culturally sensitive site
items.summary: An Aboriginal man has pleaded guilty to vandalising a culturally significant site in Victoria's Alpine National Park.

items.title: Cafe-worthy breakfast and brunch recipes for relaxed mornings
items.summary: From shakshuka to French toast, you can enjoy an indulgent breakfast or brunch at home with one of these recipes.

items.title: Hasrat Gill stumbled upon the T20 World Cup on TV one day. It changed her life forever
items.summary: State and WBBL cricketer Hasrat Gill has risen through the ranks of youth Australian cricket. She is one of many South Asian teenagers flocking to the sport.

items.title: First Nations man dies in custody at Lotus Glen Correctional Centre in Far North Queensland
items.summary: The death is not being treated as suspicious and a report is being prepared for the coroner. Fourteen Indigenous people have died in Australian prisons this year.

items.title: Hitchhiking northern quoll released back into wild in central Queensland
items.summary: Members of the public spotted the marsupial fleeing the wheel arch of a truck in Brisbane and alerted authorities.

items.title: Queensland's beleaguered DNA lab clears backlog of hundreds of sexual assault kits, director says
items.summary: Forensic Science Queensland was the subject of two commissions of inquiry, over failures in testing and cultural issues which may have led to miscarriages of justice.

items.title: Police investigate reports of hundreds of pregnant sheep stolen from Great Southern farm
items.summary: A farmer in Western Australia's Great Southern region has lost about $100,000 after someone stole hundreds of his pregnant sheep.

items.title: Western Australia will fail to achieve net-zero by 2050 on current trajectory, Woodside-funded report warns
items.summary: Woodside-commissioned modelling suggests WA is not on track to reach net zero by 2050, even if its massive Browse gas project proceeds, unless renewable energy is rolled out at unprecedented speed.

items.title: Victoria to fund free meningococcal B vaccines for Year 10 students
items.summary: The government will fund a $9 million program to vaccinate students against meningococcal B from January 1 next year.

items.title: Traffic camera captures 'spectacular' meteor breaking up over Tasmania
items.summary: Tasmanians across the state reported seeing the bright lights of a meteor last night. Astronomer Martin George says the spectacle was typical of a natural object burning up in the atmosphere.

items.title: Ambulance Victoria condemns assault on pregnant paramedic in Melbourne
items.summary: A pregnant paramedic has been assaulted in Melbourne less than a fortnight after the stabbing of a colleague, the union says.

items.title: Two mums on having babies in their 40s
items.summary: Whether it's about overcoming health challenges, waiting to meet "the right person" or wanting to be financially stable, Australian women are increasingly having children later in life.

items.title: Arsenal beats West Ham thanks to VAR call with massive implications at top and bottom of EPL ladder
items.summary: Another late goal and a controversial VAR call have helped Arsenal stay in control of the Premier League title race, while delivering another blow to relegation-threatened West Ham United.

items.title: Mary Fowler's Manchester City beats Sam Kerr's Chelsea in FA Cup semifinal, Dario Vidošić's Brighton beats Liverpool
items.summary: Mary Fowler's goal kick-started Manchester City's FA Cup comeback to down Sam Kerr's Chelsea and set up a final against Brighton, led by former Socceroo Dario Vidošić.

items.title: Paul Magnier wins stage three of Giro d'Italia 2026, Jay Vine 'lucky' to avoid serious injury in stage two crash
items.summary: Jay Vine's wife Bre takes to social media to express thanks for everyone's support following her husband's sickening fall at the Giro d'Italia.

items.title: Netanyahu says Iran's uranium must be 'taken out' before war ends— as it happened
items.summary: The Israeli prime minister says Iran's stockpile of enriched uranium must be "taken out" before the war against Iran can be considered over.

items.title: Anzac Day Bledisloe Cup Test between Wallabies and All Blacks can be a major win

items.title: AI recruited to speed up home approvals, in budget set to reshape housing market
items.summary: Artificial intelligence will be recruited to speed up assessments of housing and energy projects waiting for environmental approval, as the government races to build more homes and ward off a key frustration turning voters away from the major parties.

items.title: Inside the course targeting young tradies with get-rich schemes

items.title: Aged care patients stranded in hospitals prompt calls for government action

items.title: When is the 2026–27 federal budget? Here's what you need to know before tomorrow

items.summary: As Australian Fashion Week gets underway in Sydney, local and international designers debate the merits and detractions of using AI in the industry.

items.summary: Wellington Phoenix has become the first side from New Zealand to make an A-League grand final after inching past Brisbane Roar in their semifinal.

items.title: Fuel supplies boosted in South Australia by $40m strategic diesel reserve

items.title: Injury concern for women's world number one Aryna Sabalenka ahead of French Open
items.summary: Women's tennis world number one Aryna Sabalenka has a fortnight to recover from injury before the French Open after her loss in Rome.

items.title: Rockliff backs away from revealing reasons for backbencher Shelton legal costs
items.summary: Tasmania's premier is under fire for backtracking on an undertaking to explain why taxpayers paid a $15,000 legal bill for a backbencher.

items.title: Storm snap record losing run as Tigers coach laments 'negative smell'
items.summary: The Melbourne Storm are winners for the first time since round two, snapping a club-record losing streak with a big win over the Wests Tigers.

items.title: Rockhampton sisters raise $70,000 for Leukaemia Foundation in honour of cherished mum
items.summary: Elli Wust was pregnant with her first child when she found out her mum had a rare blood cancer. Now she has two children who never got to meet their grandmother, who died at age 58.

items.title: Love story: Love when you don't know how long you've got
items.summary: My 14-year-old daughter Pip has Williams syndrome. The future is uncertain and full of heartache, but my love for my baby is not.

items.title: Home destroyed by fire rebuilt as an Earthship made of tyres, bottles and cans
items.summary: Dee-Ann Kelly lost her home to fire in 2024, now she's rebuilding an Earthship, made from recycled materials in an effort to make her home more fire resilient.

items.title: Iran's IRGC threatens US sites as Trump awaits Tehran response — as it happened
items.summary: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps has threatened to target US sites if its tankers come under fire, Iranian media is reporting. It comes as Washington continues to wait for Tehran's response to its latest negotiating position.

items.title: Restored cider mill crushes first apples in more than 100 years
items.summary: A recently restored, hand-powered mill that revolutionised cider-making has crushed its first batch of apples in more than 100 years.

items.title: Ongoing breakdowns hamper mobile breast screening service in Tasmania, but government says fix on the way
items.summary: Angie is almost the age her mum was when she died from breast cancer, so getting a screening is important to her. But access to Tasmania's screening service has been difficult.

items.title: Jay Vine hospitalised after horror Giro d'Italia crash takes down field
items.summary: Jay Vine's awful luck continued on stage two of the Giro d'Italia with the Australian taken to hospital following a horror crash on wet roads that saw multiple riders abandon the race.

items.title: How Nigel Farage moved from the political fringe to contender for UK prime minister
items.summary: Nigel Farage has his eye on the prime minister's job himself and, if Reform can sustain this level of support at a general election, the keys to Downing Street may fall to him.

items.title: Hope for endangered Watson's tree frog as tadpoles released into wild
items.summary: Scientists hope the successful captive breeding and release of the endangered Watson's tree frog in Victoria's far east will help aid the species' survival, after its habitat was decimated during the Black Summer bushfires.

items.title: India embraces cheaper weight loss drugs but doctors are worried
items.summary: India has become one of the first countries to introduce cheaper, generic brands of weight loss drugs like Ozempic and Wegovy, but health professionals warn a lack of regulation and oversight is causing problems.

items.title: Historic Melbourne mansion Labassa 'not just a building, it's a story'
items.summary: From post-war Jewish refugees, who arrived with nothing but hope, to the bohemian artists and performers who took over in the 1970s, historic Melbourne mansion Labassa has more stories than rooms.

items.title: Ancient white wine variety reducing water bills and instilling hope for struggling Australian industry
items.summary: In an Australian first, two South Australian wine grape growers are trialling an ancient variety from Cyprus that is reducing water consumption and reconnecting them to their Mediterranean roots.

items.title: Farrer presents One Nation as a genuine electoral threat rather than just a protest
items.summary: The result in Farrer has made One Nation's attempts to become the natural home for conservative Australians look less like a protest and more like a genuine electoral threat.

items.title: Iran is trying to formalise its control over the Strait of Hormuz, but there are some obvious problems
items.summary: Amid a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran, the IRGC has launched a maritime authority for the Strait of Hormuz. The Persian Gulf Strait Authority says ships need to email their application for passage. So, we sent them a request.

items.title: Diagnosed as depression, caused by work. Who pays the cost of burnout?

items.summary: Australia's national science agency CSIRO will persist with job cuts to up to 350 roles despite an extra $387.4 million in funding announced from the federal government.

items.summary: A huge collision sees Darcy Moore concussed as Geelong takes control against Collingwood at the MCG as Gold Coast downs St Kilda in Darwin with star defender Cal Wilkie injured.

items.summary: The Iron Maiden documentary is a 50-year history of heavy metal's flashiest band as told by their biggest fans.

items.title: If you are not convinced by sardines, this is the dish to try - ABC News
items.summary: This simple and nutritious pasta dish is sure to satisfy those who already adore sardines, and it may just convert those who don't.

items.summary: Francisco Roig will think twice about betting against his new charge Iga Świątek after the veteran coach tore his Achilles tendon in a volley session in Rome.

items.title: Client abuse and unpaid labour contributing to poor vet wellbeing
items.summary: A parliamentary inquiry makes more than 30 recommendations to address increase of poor veterinary mental health outcomes.

items.title: New bug species Larrakia feather-legged assassin discovered in national park near Darwin
items.summary: A new type of bug, celebrated for the "really cool" way it assassinates prey, has been named in honour of traditional custodians in the northern Australia region where it was discovered.

items.title: The Devil Wears Prada 2 hopes you're as distracted as you are nostalgic
items.summary: Miranda Priestly has lost her fangs in the reboot as weak plot lines make room for superfluous scenes and characters.

items.title: Australia is breaking its carbon bond, but there's a cost
items.summary: As Australia weans itself away from coal, aging power stations across the country have been reduced to smoke and rubble. But for communities at the coalface, there are serious and dramatic consequences.

items.summary: Struggling Alex de Minaur suffers another early clay-court tournament exit, this time at the Italian Open in Rome.

items.title: Victoria's treaty leaders have been elected. Here's how the new structure will work
